title: Breaking Up Iran Would Be Catastrophic
content: It may suit Israel to foment separatist movements in Iran, but that doesn’t mean it serves the interests of the United States or its NATO allies. Western powers have a very poor track record with such strategies, and that would not change with a Balkanized Iran, which would produce one crisis after another.
creator: Michael Burleigh
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Iran War Is Upending Global Energy Markets
content: The longer the US-Israeli campaign against Iran lasts, the greater the implications for the global energy system. Whereas short conflicts produce volatility, prolonged instability reshapes everything from trade flows to assessments of infrastructure risk and long-term investment behavior.
creator: Carolyn Kissane
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: What Makes America Strong?
content: In 2025, US net migration was likely negative, as the number of legal immigrants has been reduced in various ways. The overall effect will be to slow economic growth – perhaps by a lot – relative to what it otherwise could be.
creator: Simon Johnson
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Birth of European Nuclear Deterrence
content: With French President Emmanuel Macron's announcement of a new doctrine of "forward deterrence," Europe is finally building a nuclear strategy that fully belongs to Europeans. The age of strategic dependence on the United States is drawing to a close.
creator: Antony Dabila
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Strengthening Women’s Rights Organizations Drives Economic Gains
content: Following foreign-aid cuts in donor countries, many developing countries have implemented austerity measures, to the detriment of women’s jobs, services, and protections. But donors and policymakers can deliver shared gains by investing in women’s rights organizations and embracing gender-based budgeting.
creator: Anisha Chugh
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Long Road to War with Iran
content: Domestic political explanations for the Trump administration's decision to go to war with Iran risk obscuring deeper causes. In fact, the final decision appeared to be sudden only because the US, Israel, and Gulf countries' strategic trajectories had been converging for nearly a decade.
creator: Carla Norrlöf
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: When Is an Illegal War Morally Defensible?
content: US President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu flagrantly violated international law by starting a war in Iran. But while historical examples of legitimacy trumping legality are few and far between, they do exist.
creator: Gareth Evans
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Who Can Lead India Toward Sustainable Development?
content: India's efforts to expand female representation in government are welcome, and they may well help to advance sustainable development. But how many women hold office matters less than which women do – and whom they represent.
creator: Bipasha Maity
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Paradox of Democratic Backsliding
content: Around the world, authoritarian and populist leaders are eroding civil liberties, weakening democratic checks and balances, and browbeating the press and the judiciary; but rather than taking to the streets in protest, many voters apparently love it. What explains this apparent political masochism?
creator: Andrés Velasco
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Trump’s Unaffordable Midterm Stimulus
content: A new report from the Congressional Budget Office shows that the US fiscal outlook has deteriorated sharply since Donald Trump returned to the White house last year. But rather than addressing America's shaky public finances, Trump is courting disaster by devising ways to juice the economy before November's midterm elections.
creator: Desmond Lachman
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Magical Thinking Won’t Make Europe an AI Power
content: As long as the European Union remains dependent on a handful of US tech companies, its ambitions to become a global leader in AI will remain out of reach. In today’s geopolitical landscape, strategic autonomy is defined by ownership and control of critical infrastructure.
creator: Cristina Caffarra
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: How Protectionism Sank American Shipping
content: More than a century of shielding domestic shipbuilding and shipping has left the industry less competitive than ever. President Donald Trump’s latest effort to revitalize it risks making matters worse by driving up trade costs and entrenching the very structural weaknesses it purports to fix.
creator: Anne O. Krueger
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Reimagining the Economics of Culture
content: Standard economic frameworks and metrics for assessing economic output have systematically undervalued the dynamic, long-run returns that culture and the arts generate. Major cultural events like Brazil’s Carnival highlight this failure – and offer a powerful corrective to it.
creator: Mariana Mazzucato
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Preparing for AI-Enabled Bioweapons
content: The prospect of bioweapons developed by non-state actors has lent new urgency to multilateral pandemic preparation and response efforts. Yet at a time when AI has made such threats exponentially greater, the funding model that sustains critical health-security safeguards is collapsing.
creator: Sania Nishtar
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: America’s Latest Middle-East Fiasco
content: US President Donald Trump did not decide to go to war against Iran because he had run out of diplomatic options, or because an orderly regime change had become realistic, or because military strikes would neutralize any imminent security threat. He launched another US war in the Middle East – toppling a core campaign promise to his MAGA base – to advance his own personal and political interests. Iranians and people throughout the region will pay a high price, but so could Americans if Trump’s lawlessness abroad advances his authoritarian ambitions at home.
creator: PS Commentators
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Big Picture
content: <html><head></head><body></body></html>
creator: PS editors
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Afghan Women, Too
content: The Taliban’s war on women continues to plumb new lows with the enactment of a criminal procedure code that fails to criminalize most forms of gender violence. On International Women’s Day, the world should recognize, commend, and support the organizations inside Afghanistan that continue to stand up for gender equality.
creator: Gordon Brown
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Why Attack Iran?
content: The Trump administration’s propaganda about its foreign policy motivations for starting a regime-change war in Iran strains credulity. Instead, the surrounding evidence implies that the US attack could very well be about undermining American democracy, enriching Donald Trump, or both.
creator: Timothy Snyder
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Saving Innovation From the Anti-Vaxxers
content: With a hostile political atmosphere making investors increasingly wary of vaccine-related research in the US, firms are winding down development programs, shelving manufacturing plans, and laying off workers. While these responses are understandable, they are short-sighted.
creator: Christopher Marquis
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Baby Bump From Remote Work
content: A new study across 38 countries found that among adults aged 20-45 who work from home at least one day per week, actual births since 2023 and planned family size are higher. This implies that an increase in remote work would boost fertility much more effectively than expensive pronatalist policies.
creator: Cevat Giray Aksoy
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page
